claim: The key shift is an ecosystem play: companies need to build 'frontier intelligence' by combining any company's choice of model, tools, data, and a harness—not just consuming one model.
actionable: If you are building AI products, design them around model-agnostic harnesses and tool frameworks; enable customers to choose their own models and build proprietary expertise with evals and context layers.
source_title: Satya Nadella on AI at Microsoft Build 2026

claim: Clean data lineage, ablation studies, and cognitive core identification are critical for building strong foundation models; many open-weight models look good on one or two benchmarks but fail in practice.
actionable: When evaluating or training foundation models, insist on understanding the data lineage and ablation process; don't rely on single-benchmark performance; test on diverse real-world tasks.
source_title: Satya Nadella on AI at Microsoft Build 2026

claim: Hill-climbing scaffolds around foundation models allow companies to build specialist models without retraining; combining a small model (5B) with RL, traces, and private evals can achieve frontier-level performance on company-specific tasks.
actionable: Build evaluation and fine-tuning infrastructure around a chosen foundation model; use RL and trace collection to hill-climb toward better performance on your domain; this scales cheaper than retraining.
source_title: Satya Nadella on AI at Microsoft Build 2026

claim: Real-world evaluation is more important than benchmark saturation; true evals happen when users can do unique things they value; token optimization matters because you want to deliver value at every step of token usage.
actionable: Define success by real user outcomes, not benchmark scores; measure token efficiency in delivering value; optimize for delivering measurable business or user outcomes, not maximum capability.
source_title: Satya Nadella on AI at Microsoft Build 2026

claim: Private evals are the highest-value IP: if you can switch models while maintaining performance via your harness and evals, you have control; if you can't, you are locked in.
actionable: Build and protect private evaluation suites specific to your domain; regularly test whether you can swap foundation models without performance degradation; this is your control lever.
source_title: Satya Nadella on AI at Microsoft Build 2026

claim: The harness (model + tools + context + data loop) matters as much as the model itself; a well-designed multi-model harness can outperform single models and find vulnerabilities (as with M-Dash security copilot).
actionable: When evaluating AI systems, measure the full harness performance, not just model benchmarks; invest heavily in context layer engineering, tool selection, and feedback loops.
source_title: Satya Nadella on AI at Microsoft Build 2026

claim: Coding agents drive IDE/UI transformation: the cognitive load of managing 100+ agent sessions forces new paradigms beyond chat-only interfaces, including canvas-based multi-artifact editing.
actionable: If you are building tools for coding agents or agentic workflows, design for multi-artifact management and high cognitive load; chat-only UX will become a bottleneck.
source_title: Satya Nadella on AI at Microsoft Build 2026

claim: Long-running enterprise autopilots (delegated authority agents) create value by compressing workflows and completing glue work; the future is humans defining goals and agents executing with traced decisions.
actionable: Design agent workflows around human-defined objectives and delegated authority; log all agent decisions and traces so humans can audit and improve over time; this scales human judgment.
source_title: Satya Nadella on AI at Microsoft Build 2026

claim: Work IQ transforms M365 data (email, meetings, documents) into a queryable database for agents; previously locked data now becomes a competitive advantage when exposed as a knowledge layer.
actionable: Audit your enterprise data assets (email, docs, meetings); design interfaces and agents to make this data accessible and queryable; this becomes a moat against competitors.
source_title: Satya Nadella on AI at Microsoft Build 2026

claim: Token capital and human capital compound together; company IP increasingly lives in agent traces and evals, not just human expertise; these should be tracked on the balance sheet as proprietary assets.
actionable: Treat your private evals, agent traces, and model fine-tuning datasets as balance-sheet assets; invest in infrastructure to capture, version, and secure this IP.
source_title: Satya Nadella on AI at Microsoft Build 2026

claim: SaaS unbundling/rebundling is happening: traditional SaaS bundles (data model + business logic + UI) are being decomposed; new business models emerge around exposing data (not apps) to agents.
actionable: If you are in SaaS, prepare for disaggregation; design so your core data model and business logic can be separated from the UI; enable agents to query and modify data directly.
source_title: Satya Nadella on AI at Microsoft Build 2026

claim: Pricing models will evolve: per-user bundling (from subscription certainty) will coexist with consumption and outcome-based pricing; outcome pricing is attractive until realized, then customers switch back to fixed costs.
actionable: As an AI platform vendor, offer flexible pricing: per-user for budget certainty, consumption for scaling, and outcome-based for high-trust partnerships; be prepared to switch as customer comfort changes.
source_title: Satya Nadella on AI at Microsoft Build 2026

claim: Engineering roles will consolidate: full-stack builders, FTEs/forward-deployment engineers, security engineers, and large-scale infrastructure engineers will emerge; narrow specialist roles will merge into agentic workflows.
actionable: If you are hiring engineers, restructure roles around full-stack capability and domain expertise; reduce the number of narrow specialties; invest in infrastructure and security engineering.
source_title: Satya Nadella on AI at Microsoft Build 2026

claim: Making the impossible possible requires reconceptualizing work itself, not just automating existing workflows; the Azure networking team reframed 'managing fiber' to 'building the agentic system that manages fiber.'
actionable: When designing agent systems, encourage teams to redefine their work at a higher level of abstraction; shift from 'doing X' to 'building the system that does X'; this unlocks new leverage.
source_title: Satya Nadella on AI at Microsoft Build 2026

claim: Community permission for large-scale infrastructure (datacenters, energy) requires tangible benefits: job creation, tax base improvement, energy price reductions, and water replenishment; skepticism from communities is healthy and should be earned.
actionable: When scaling AI infrastructure, proactively invest in community benefits: local hiring, workforce training, transparent energy/water impact reporting; earn permission through measurable outcomes, not promises.
source_title: Satya Nadella on AI at Microsoft Build 2026

claim: Education is ripe for innovation: new pedagogies, credentials, and employment pathways are needed; the next big startup might be a new university model that teaches AI-era skills and connects graduates to economic opportunity.
actionable: If you are thinking about education, design for continuous learning and AI-native skills; rethink credentials to reflect actual capability rather than seat time; build direct pathways to economic opportunity.
source_title: Satya Nadella on AI at Microsoft Build 2026
